'''Sitaleki Akauola''' (born 7 April 1992) is a [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tongan]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

He previously played for the [[Wests Tigers]] and the [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[NRL]]. Akauola has also played for [[Warrington Wolves]] and [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]], spending time on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Rochdale Hornets]], and at [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Akauola was born in [[Manurewa]], New Zealand, to parents Siupeli and Christina.<ref>{{cite web|last=Pengilly|first=Adam|date=24 August 2016|title=Penrith Panthers' Sitaleki Akauola emerges from dark times to be an NRL force|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/penrith-panthers/penrith-panthers-sitaleki-akauola-emerges-from-dark-times-to-be-an-nrl-force-20160822-gqya4r.html|publisher=Sydney Morning Herald}}</ref>

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Manurewa Marlins]], and was in the [[New Zealand Warriors]] junior system before being signed by the [[Wests Tigers]].<ref name=tigers1>{{cite web |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2014/09/01/akauolas-re-signing-makes-huge-impact/ |title=Akauola's re-signing makes huge impact |publisher=NRL |date=1 September 2014 |access-date=1 May 2022}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Wests Tigers===
In 2012, Akauola played for the Wests Tigers' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://nycdatabase.org/players/a/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140413110053/http://nycdatabase.org/players/a/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=13 April 2014|title=A|work=NYC DATABASE|accessdate=24 September 2015}}</ref><ref name="weststigers.com.au">{{cite news|url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2014/07/29/akauola_re_signs_with_wests_tigers.html|title=Akauola re-signs with Wests Tigers - Tigers|publisher=Wests Tigers|date=29 July 2014|accessdate=26 September 2015}}</ref> "The Auckland Rugby League from New Zealand got me a trial with the Tigers out in Campbelltown. Two weeks after that I had a trial with NYC," he said.<ref name="nrl.com">{{cite news|website=NRL.com|title=Akauola's re-signing makes huge impact|first=Matt|last=Encarnacion|url=http://www.nrl.com/akauolas-re-signing-makes-huge-impact/tabid/10874/newsid/81475/default.aspx|accessdate=1 September 2014|date=1 September 2014}}</ref> He played from the bench and set up two tries as the [[Wests Tigers]] won the [[2012 NRL Under-20s season results#Grand final|2012 NYC Grand Final]].<ref>{{cite news|website=rleague.com|title=Wests Tigers 2012 NYC Toyota Cup Premiers|url=http://rleague.com/news/Wests-Tigers-2012-NYC-Toyota-Cup-Premiers_75954/|access-date=3 October 2012|date=3 October 2012|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140903113313/http://rleague.com/news/Wests-Tigers-2012-NYC-Toyota-Cup-Premiers_75954/|archive-date=3 September 2014}}</ref>

In Round 5 of the [[2013 NRL season]], Akauola made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the [[Wests Tigers]] against the [[Melbourne Storm]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2013/round-5/melbourne-vs-wests-tigers/summary.html|title=NRL 2013|author=Shawn Dollin and Andrew Ferguson|accessdate=24 September 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://rleague.com/players/Sitaleki-Akauola_3946/Matches/|title=Sitaleki Akauola player results, scores, news and statistics|website=Rleague.com|access-date=21 September 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140407081944/http://rleague.com/players/Sitaleki-Akauola_3946/Matches/|archive-date=7 April 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref> It would be his only appearance for the season.<ref name=tigers1/>

On 29 July 2014, Akauola re-signed with the [[Wests Tigers]] on a two-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/akauola-re-signs-with-wests-tigers-14282/|title=Akauola re-signs with Wests Tigers|work=NRL - Zero Tackle|accessdate=24 September 2015}}</ref><ref name="weststigers.com.au"/> Mid-season, he played a string of games from the bench and in the second row. Towards the end of the season, he played four games on the wing, showing his versatility.<ref>{{cite web|author=Shawn Dollin and Andrew Ferguson|date=29 August 2015|accessdate=24 September 2015|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTMtMTY0NC00LS0tLS0tMjAzNTEtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List}}</ref>

===Penrith Panthers===
Akauola signed a contract with the [[Penrith Panthers]] in September 2015, after not playing any first grade games with the Tigers that year.<ref>{{cite news|author=Danny Weidler|url=http://www.smh.com.au/sport/us-sports/jarryd-hayne-humility-leads-to-big-fouryear-deal-with-hedge-fund-company-20150919-gjqj88.html|title=Jarryd Hayne humility leads to big four-year deal with financial company|work=Sydney Morning Herald|date=20 September 2015|accessdate=26 September 2015}}</ref>

In 2017, Akauola featured in both the Penrith NRL and [[NSW Cup]] squads. Akauola was a part of Penrith's 2017 NSW Cup Championship team, and the 2017 State Championship team, helping defeat the [[Wyong Roos]] and [[Papua New Guinea Hunters]] respectively.

===Warrington Wolves===
In August 2017, Akauola signed a two-year contract with the [[Warrington Wolves]] starting in 2018.<ref>{{cite web|date=19 August 2017|title=Penrith Panthers utility player Sitaleki Akauola signs with English club Warrington.|url=http://wwos.nine.com.au/2017/08/19/09/52/penrith-has-lost-talented-utility-player-sitaleki-akauola-to-english-club-warrington|publisher=Wide World of Sports|accessdate=19 August 2017}}</ref>

He played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|publisher=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|accessdate=1 November 2019}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
On 23 October 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=23 Oct 2021|accessdate=26 Dec 2021|title=Salford confirm signing of Sitaleki Akauola|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-confirm-signing-of-sitaleki-akauola/}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers===
On 17 November 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=17 Nov 2024|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former Super League and NRL powerhouse makes cross-Championship switch for 2025|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-super-league-and-nrl-powerhouse-makes-cross-championship-switch-for-2025}}</ref>
